title: Mildly Peppery Saudi Arabian Cucumber Wrap
description: A lazy sunday saudi-arabian dish featuring persian cucumber and ssam-wrap, perfect for a satvik-friendly meal. This dish is peppery, crunchy, and easy to make.
instructions: Slice the persian cucumber into thin rounds and set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together the lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Brush the ssam-wrap with the lemon juice mixture, making sure to cover the entire surface.
Arrange a few slices of cucumber on each ssam-wrap, leaving a small border around the edges.
Sprinkle chopped parsley and scallions over the cucumber slices.
Fold the bottom edge of the ssam-wrap up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the wrap to form a tight cylinder.
You can also store the wraps in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 hours before serving.
Let the wraps come to room temperature before serving.
